title: I tried to use podman update to add a new device to the running container, but it didn't take effect and there was no error feedback. Is this completely unfeasible? Or is there something wrong with my approach?
body:
### Issue Description


```
str, err := containers.Update(ctx, &entities.ContainerUpdateOptions{
		NameOrID: "18bd9bbc15a7",
		Specgen: &specgen.SpecGenerator{
			ContainerResourceConfig: specgen.ContainerResourceConfig{
				ResourceLimits: &spec.LinuxResources{
                                		Devices: []spec.LinuxDeviceCgroup{
                                			{
                                				Allow:  true,
                                				Type:   "c",
                                				Major:  &[]int64{226}[0],
                                				Minor:  &[]int64{130}[0],
                                				Access: "rwm",
                                			},
                                			{
                                				Allow:  true,
                                				Type:   "c",
                                				Major:  &[]int64{195}[0],
                                				Minor:  &[]int64{2}[0],
                                				Access: "rwm",
                                			},
                                			{
                                				Allow:  true,
                                				Type:   "c",
                                				Major:  &[]int64{226}[0],
                                				Minor:  &[]int64{3}[0],
                                				Access: "rwm",
                                			},
                                		},
                                	},
			},
		},
	})
```
This is my host environment:
user@host:~$ stat -fc %T /sys/fs/cgroup/
cgroup2fs
user@host:~$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.20.2
Built:        Fri Mar 24 16:08:18 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
user@host:~$ crun --version
crun version 1.8.3
commit: 59f2beb7efb0d35611d5818fd0311883676f6f7e
rundir: /run/user/1000/crun
spec: 1.0.0
+SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL

### Steps to reproduce the issue

just run code like in the issue desc



### Describe the results you received

I checked  podman inspect, containers/storage/overlay-containers/b305d8da52a8717319bb23244d18291afdc5f707e0a0957747b4fbd53dbe5d71/userdata/config.json, but don't see anything about my newly added device.I went into the container to check, and I didn't see any new devices appearing in the /dev directory

### Describe the results you expected

Hope the newly added device will appear in the container.
I want to be able to add a device to a running container. If this is not possible at all, please specify it too.

no more

comments.body: No errors are returned and no changes occur.
No devices will be added, only thing that takes effect on an update are cgroup changes, as I understand it.
We probably ought to add a warning if any fields of the spec we don't support are passed. Should be easy, given we don't really support anything beyond resource limits.
Thanks, I think I got the concept wrong.Adding some legitimacy to the resources file might avoid some usage confusion.
